title: Slope Stability Inclinometer Array with LoRa Early-Warning Telemetry
description: Deploys a three-sensor borehole MEMS inclinometer array on an unstable hillside, computes real-time factor of safety using the infinite slope equation, transmits FS and tilt data every minute via LoRa to a remote base station, and activates a local alarm when FS drops below 1.5 — targeting earthwork geotechnical failure, distinct from bridge vibration projects.
objective: Early landslide warning with FS<1.5 LoRa alert, local alarm, and hourly SD displacement trend log; detect freeze-thaw destabilisation via soil temperature.
constraints: Arduino Mega, three MPU-6050 with TCA9548A mux, SX1276 LoRa, DS18B20 probes, SD card; remote hillside, LiPo+solar; φ=30°, c=5 kPa, z=1.5 m.
supporting_fact_titles: Factor of Safety for Infinite Slope Stability
design_element_titles: MEMS Tilt and Inclinometer Array via I2C Mux

element_usage_notes.MEMS Tilt and Inclinometer Array via I2C: Three sensors at 150 mm intervals; mean tilt used as slope angle β input to FS equation.
element_usage_notes.LoRa SX1276 Remote Telemetry Node: Sends FS, tilt array, and soil temperature every 60 s to base station.
element_usage_notes.Buzzer and LED Fault Annunciator: Local on-site warning at FS<1.5; fast beep at FS<1.2 (imminent failure).
element_usage_notes.DS18B20 1-Wire Temperature Probe Array: Soil temperature monitors freeze-thaw cycles that reduce effective cohesion.

element_interactions.from: MEMS Tilt and Inclinometer Array via I2C Mux
element_interactions.description: Tilt angle array and computed FS packaged into LoRa payload.
Soil temperature included in telemetry packet for freeze-thaw correlation.

element_interactions.from: MEMS Tilt and Inclinometer Array via I2C Mux
element_interactions.description: FS below 1.5 triggers warning; below 1.2 triggers critical alarm.
